checks.title: Agent context file present (AGENTS.md)
checks.remediation: Create an AGENTS.md at the repository root describing what the project is, how to build/test it, and the conventions agents must follow.

checks.title: Agent context file is substantive
checks.evidence: AGENTS.md: 79 non-empty lines, 17 headings (needs ≥20 lines and ≥2 headings).
checks.remediation: Flesh out AGENTS.md: add sections for project overview, build & test commands, architecture, and conventions (aim for 20+ meaningful lines with headings).

checks.title: Scoped rules in use
checks.evidence: No scoped rule files found (.cursor/rules, .windsurf/rules, .clinerules, .continue/rules, .github/instructions, .agents/rules, nested AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, …).
checks.remediation: Add at least one scoped rule file for your AI tool (e.g. .cursor/rules/*.mdc, .windsurf/rules/*.md, .clinerules/*.md) or a nested AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md in a subdirectory, stating the project non-negotiables.

checks.title: Rules have valid frontmatter
checks.remediation: Give every rule activation metadata in frontmatter (description, globs/trigger/paths/applyTo, or alwaysApply) so the agent knows when to load it.

checks.title: Rules are scoped, not all always-on
checks.remediation: Scope rules to paths (globs, trigger glob, paths, applyTo) instead of making everything always-on — blanket rules consume context on every request.

checks.title: No bloated rules (≤500 lines each)
checks.remediation: Split rules longer than 500 lines into focused, scoped rules or move procedural content into a skill — huge rules crowd out task context.

checks.title: README present
checks.remediation: Add a README.md — agents (and humans) use it as the first orientation document.

checks.title: No legacy .cursorrules file
checks.remediation: Migrate the legacy .cursorrules file to scoped rules with frontmatter (.cursor/rules/*.mdc or your tool equivalent) — .cursorrules is deprecated.

checks.title: At least one agent skill defined
checks.remediation: Create a SKILL.md under your tool skills directory (.cursor/skills/, .claude/skills/, or .agents/skills/) packaging a procedural workflow the agent should follow on demand.

checks.title: Skills declare name and description
checks.evidence: All 9 skill(s) declare name and description.
checks.remediation: Add frontmatter with name: and description: to every SKILL.md — the agent decides whether to load a skill from those two fields alone.

checks.title: Explicit workflows/commands defined
checks.remediation: Add explicit workflow/command entry points (.cursor/commands/, .windsurf/workflows/, .claude/commands/, .continue/prompts/, …) for workflows you trigger intentionally.

checks.title: Skill descriptions are trigger-worthy
checks.remediation: Write skill descriptions of 40+ characters that say when to use the skill ("Use when…"), not just what it is — vague descriptions never trigger.

checks.title: Custom subagent defined
checks.remediation: Create a subagent definition (.cursor/agents/, .claude/agents/, or .opencode/agents/) for a purpose-built delegate (planning, review, release…).

checks.title: Subagents declare name and description
checks.evidence: All 4 subagent(s) declare name and description.
checks.remediation: Add frontmatter with name: and description: to every subagent definition — the parent agent decides whether to delegate from those two fields alone.

checks.title: Hooks configuration present and valid JSON
checks.remediation: Create a hooks configuration (.cursor/hooks.json or .claude/settings.json hooks key) — hooks are the harness layer that can observe and control the agent loop deterministically.

checks.title: Hooks use known events and a version field
checks.remediation: Register handlers only on documented events for your tool (Cursor: beforeShellExecution, afterFileEdit, …; Claude Code: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, …) — typos fail silently.

checks.title: Gate hook guards risky operations
checks.remediation: Register a gate hook (Cursor: beforeShellExecution / beforeMCPExecution / preToolUse; Claude Code: PreToolUse) that returns allow/deny/ask for destructive operations.

checks.title: Feedback hook observes agent output
checks.remediation: Register a feedback hook (Cursor: afterFileEdit / postToolUse / stop; Claude Code: PostToolUse) — e.g. auto-format edited files or run a quick lint.

checks.title: Hook scripts exist in the repository
checks.remediation: Commit the scripts referenced by your hooks config — a hook pointing at a missing script fails open on every machine but yours.

checks.title: Test runner configured
checks.remediation: Wire up a test runner (vitest/jest, pytest, go test, cargo test…) with a standard entry point — tests are the strongest feedback sensor an agent can run on its own work.

checks.title: Linter configured
checks.remediation: Add a linter config (eslint/biome, ruff, golangci-lint, clippy.toml, rubocop…) — linters give the agent instant, deterministic feedback on every edit.

checks.title: Type checking in place
checks.remediation: Enable static type checking (tsconfig.json with strict: true, mypy/pyright for Python) — typed code is dramatically more harnessable: the compiler catches agent mistakes for free.

checks.title: Formatter configured
checks.remediation: Add an auto-formatter (prettier/biome, black or ruff format, gofmt/rustfmt are built-in) — formatting noise in diffs hides real agent mistakes from review.

checks.title: Test files actually exist
checks.remediation: Write at least one real test file — a configured runner with zero tests gives the agent a green light it did not earn.

checks.title: CI pipeline configured
checks.remediation: Add a CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) — CI is the harness sensor that catches what slipped past local checks, on every push.

checks.title: CI runs the test suite
checks.remediation: Make CI execute the test suite (npm test, pytest, go test…) so no agent-authored change can merge without the sensors firing.

checks.title: CI runs lint / type checks
checks.remediation: Add lint and typecheck steps to CI — cheap computational sensors belong on every push ("keep quality left").

checks.title: Pre-commit checks installed
checks.remediation: Add pre-commit tooling (husky + lint-staged, pre-commit, lefthook) so fast checks run before a commit exists — the earliest possible feedback loop.

checks.title: .gitignore present
checks.remediation: Add a .gitignore — agents commit what they see; make sure build output and local state are invisible.

checks.title: .gitignore covers environment files
checks.remediation: Add ".env" and ".env.*" to .gitignore so an agent can never stage credentials by accident.

checks.title: No unprotected .env files in the tree
checks.remediation: Remove committed .env files (keep only .env.example) or at minimum ensure .gitignore excludes them — env files in an agent's working tree leak into context and commits.

checks.title: MCP configuration free of credentials
checks.evidence: No MCP config in repository (nothing to leak).
checks.remediation: Never inline API keys in MCP config (.cursor/mcp.json, .mcp.json, .agents/mcp_config.json) — use ${VAR} interpolation and document required variables in .env.example.

checks.title: License present
checks.evidence: No LICENSE file at repository root.
checks.remediation: Add a LICENSE file — required for open-source distribution and plugin marketplaces.

checks.title: No credential signatures in harness files
checks.evidence: Scanned 15 harness file(s); no credential signatures.
checks.remediation: Remove any API keys or tokens from AGENTS.md, rules, and hooks configuration — harness files are loaded into model context on every session.

checks.title: Dependency lockfile committed
checks.remediation: Commit the lockfile (package-lock.json, uv.lock, Cargo.lock…) — reproducible installs mean the agent's sensors run against the same dependencies everywhere.

checks.title: MCP config uses env interpolation for credentials
checks.remediation: Reference credential-shaped values in MCP config via ${ENV_VAR} interpolation instead of literals — this rewards deliberate, safe tool-access configuration.
